entry.title: SAP Build Code at Devtoberfest by SAP TechEd 2024
entry.content:
Hey everyone!

Devtoberfest is almost here - and it's Devtoberfest by SAP TechEd in 2024! 🍻

This year, Devtoberfest, the celebration of SAP technologies, is getting closer to SAP's premiere tech conference, SAP TechEd. The free, virtual event will run from September 23 to October 25, 2024, with a break during the week of SAP TechEd Virtual 2024. All sessions will be livestreamed/premiered on the SAP Developers' channel on YouTube.  

 

You can find a list of all the session content in the SAP Community events area: Devtoberfest Events.

  • Monday: 🟢 ABAP & CAP
  • Tuesday: 🟣 Tooling (for SAP Build Apps and SAP Build Code)
  • Wednesday: 🔴 Integration
  • Thursday: 🔵 Machine Learning, AI, and Data
  • Friday: 🟠 Frontend

 

Below is a detailed overview of the SAP Build Code sessions, including abstracts, schedules, speakers, and links. 
ℹ️ Click on the session titles to navigate to the respective event pages in the dedicated SAP Community Group, where you can already start communicating with the speakers, your peers, and the event organizers. Indicate your interest in the session in the "Will you be attending?" and use the "Add to Calendar" option to save the date and time to your individual calendar.
🔔 You can also use the "Notify me" feature on YouTube to be notified when livestreams and premieres start!

Enjoy the event! 🍻

 

 

🟣 Develop a Full-Stack CAP Application with SAP Build Code

Learn how to generate a complete, full-stack CAP application, including sample data, with a single prompt using Joule in SAP Build Code. Enhance the application with additional functionality using custom handlers and CAP plugins.

Speaker: Daniel Schlachter (SAP)
📅 Sept 24, 2024 | ⏰ 11:00 A.M - 12:PM AM CEST

Add to Calender

 

 

🟣 Cross-Platform Mobile App Development with SAP Build Code and the MDK

Learn how to create and enhance cross-platform enterprise mobile applications for both iOS and Android using Mobile Development Kit (MDK). This session covers getting started by creating your first app from the SAP Build Lobby and enhancing it in the Build Code development environment. You will learn how to leverage a single code base to deliver robust and visually appealing applications. The session will also introduce new debugging tools to monitor logs and network traces. By the end of the session, you will be equipped with the skills to build, deploy, and maintain scalable and efficient mobile applications and to tailor them to your enterprise's unique needs using SAP Build Code.

Speaker: Bill Froelich (SAP)
📅 Oct 1, 2024 | ⏰ 6:00 P.M CEST- 7:00 PM CEST

Add to Calender

 

 

🟠 Build Elegant Enterprise Apps for Android with SAP Fiori and SAP Build Code

Learn to design and create enterprise mobile applications for the Android operating system. This session begins with an idea for an app and data, demonstrating how to apply SAP Fiori for Android Design Guidelines to design an effective and visually appealing user interface. Using Jetpack Compose and SAP BTP SDK for Android, we will guide you through the development process, ensuring your app runs smoothly across various Android devices and form factors. By the end of the session, you'll have a clear understanding of how to design and build robust, scalable, and functional Android applications tailored to your enterprise needs.

Speaker: Natalie Stainton (SAP) , Mark Fogle (SAP)
📅 Oct 4, 2024 | ⏰ 5:30 P.M CEST- 6:30 PM CEST

Add to Calender

 

 

🟠 Craft Enterprise Apps for iOS, iPadOS, and visionOS with SAP Fiori and SAP Build Code

Explore how to design and develop enterprise mobile applications for Apple platforms, including iOS, iPadOS, and visionOS. This session begins with an idea for an app and data, demonstrating how to apply SAP Fiori for iOS Design Guidelines to create an intuitive and visually compelling user interface. Leveraging SwiftUI and SAP BTP SDK for iOS, we will demonstrate the development process, ensuring your app delivers a seamless experience across various devices. By the end of the session, you will be equipped with the knowledge to build sophisticated, scalable, and efficient applications tailored to your enterprise's unique needs.

Speaker: Huy Ngo (SAP) , Sheng Xu (SAP)
📅 Oct 4, 2024 | ⏰ 7:00 P.M CEST- 8:00 PM CEST

Add to Calender

 

 

Have I aroused your curiosity? Then attend the various sessions to learn more about the latest and greatest SAP Build Code innovations and best practices.

You can also check out the SAP Build Code Sessions at SAP TechEd in 2024

We look forward to interacting with you!

entry.title: SAP Build Code Sessions at SAP TechEd in 2024
entry.content:
Hello Everyone !

That time of year is here again, SAP TechEd 2024 is just around the corner! 

 

SAP TechEd is the leading tech conference for all developers, IT professionals, business experts, tech visionaries, innovators, and leaders eager to gain in-depth knowledge of SAP’s solutions, platforms, and technologies, along with insights into their future direction. It also offer an opportunity to network and connect with peers and SAP experts. 

SAP TechEd in 2024 will kick off with a free, engaging virtual event accessible to everyone, followed by SAP TechEd on Tour stops all around the world for a unique opportunity for more in-depth, in-person learning experiences. The series of local on-site events will be organized in partnership with and hosted by SAP User Groups and SAPinsider. Learn more about the virtual experience here.

The choice is yours: Join us virtually for the first event in the series, SAP TechEd Virtual 2024! 

📌Save the date and register now!
📅 October 8 - 9, 2024

At this year's SAP TechEd, you will have the opportunity to attend a variety of sessions and formats - such as lectures, jumpstarts, and roadmaps - organized into six (6) content tracks that are aligned with your business needs, covering SAP technologies, products, and solutions:

  • Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP (DT)
  • SAP Business Technology Platform – General (XP)
  • Application Development and Automation (AD)
  • Data and Analytics (DA)
  • Integration (IN)
  • Artificial Intelligence (AI)

 

📚Access the full session catalog of SAP TechEd Virtual 2024

 

SAP Build Code - Sessions

 

📅Tuesday, Oct 8

 

1. AD100 | Unlock SAP Build and ABAP Cloud interoperability to elevate your extensions

[Track Overview, 45 min] Discover a new approach to building extensions while keeping your core clean. Learn how SAP Build solutions and the ABAP Cloud development model work together to foster fusion development. Whether you prefer pro-code or low-code tools, you can smoothly integrate them to build extensions tailored to your needs. Learn from experts about best practices, innovative solutions, and practical applications that can enhance your development processes and drive business growth.
Speakers: Dr. Alexander Rother (SAP) , Bjorn Schulz (SAP) , Andre Borchert (SAP)
Schedule: 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 6:00 PM – 6:45 PM CEST​

 

2. AD105 | Enhance your fusion team collaboration with ABAP Cloud and SAP Build

[Lecture, 25 min] Learn how to take advantage of interoperability between SAP’s low-code and pro-code solutions. Discover how you can use the graphical modeler in the development environment of SAP Build Code for the ABAP Cloud development model and how your ABAP Cloud projects can be integrated into the lobby of SAP Build solutions.
Speakers: Tim Back (SAP) , Anne Keller (SAP) 
Schedule: 📅 Tuesday, Oct 8 | 🕛 7:00 PM – 7:25 PM CEST​

 

📅Wednesday, Oct 9

 

3. AD104 | SAP Build Code: AI development and new service integrations with CAP

[Lecture, 25 min] Examine new capabilities of SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) in SAP Build Code, and experience how the Joule copilot can speed up your extension development. In addition, find out how service integrations with CAP can reduce development effort, help you avoid pitfalls, and allow for evolution without disruption following the clean core strategy.
Speakers: David Kunz (SAP) , Daniel Schlachter (SAP) 
Schedule: 📅 Wednesday, Oct 9 | 🕛 11:30 AM – 11:55 AM CEST​

 

4. AD101 | Tailor your UI with SAPUI5 in SAP Build Code

[Lecture, 25 min] Discover the latest on SAPUI5 development in SAP Build Code, providing a modern user experience across ABAP and SAP Cloud Application Programming Model. Learn how to realize clean core extensibility with new capabilities and how you can future-proof your app with new tools and best practices. Understand why SAP Web Components are strategic and how you can benefit from using them.
Speakers: Oliver Graeff (SAP) , Peter Muessig (SAP) 
Schedule: 📅 Wednesday, Oct 9 | 🕛 12:00 PM – 12:25 PM CEST​

 

5. AD103 | Extend your SAP solutions with custom mobile apps using SAP Build Code

[Lecture, 25 min] Leverage our mobile development kit, SAP BTP SDK for iOS, and SAP BTP SDK for Android to create extensible enterprise-grade mobile apps. Learn how you can transform your user experience with SAP Mobile Services and AI capabilities in SAP Build Code.

Speakers: Sami Lechner (SAP) , Sandeep Tetali (SAP) 
Schedule: 📅 Wednesday, Oct 9 | 🕛 12:30 PM – 12:55 PM CEST​

 

📅Thursday, Oct 10

 

6. AD181 | Develop applications and processes with SAP Build and Joule

[Jump-Start, 25 min] Immerse yourself in creating comprehensive intelligent applications using SAP Build solutions in this hands-on session. Gain comprehensive knowledge of full-stack development, process automation, and business site creation with SAP Build. Learn how SAP Cloud Application Programming Model is leveraged in a highly productive development environment with Joule technology.

Speakers: Eric Solberg (SAP) , Patrick Schad (SAP) 
Schedule: 📅 Thursday, Oct 10 | 🕛 9:00 AM – 9:25 AM CEST​

 

 

I hope this overview gives you a few more reasons to join us at SAP TechEd 2024.

We look forward to meeting and interacting with you virtually at SAP TechEd Virtual and in person at the local events in West Palm Beach (US), Melbourne (AUS), Copenhagen (DK), Birmingham (UK), and Wiesbaden (DE).

In the meantime, you can check out this overview of the SAP Build Code Session at Devtoberfest which will take place virtually from Sept 23 to Oct 25, 2024.

Stay tuned - and see you there!

entry.title: SAP BTP SDK for iOS 24.8.0 is now available
entry.content:
Release announcement: SAP BTP SDK for iOS 24.8.0 can be downloaded

The new version of SAP BTP SDK for iOS 24.8.0 is now generally available.

You can download the latest version of the SDK either from the Software Center or the downloads section on develeopers.sap.com

With this latest release, the SAP BTP SDK for iOS team has brought some exciting new features for you to explore.

In this blog, we will highlight the new upgrade's offerings. Please visit our documentation and API reference to get a detailed overview of all the features released.

 

Highlights

 

visionOS App Development

In the previous release (24.4), we introduced the foundational capability to start developing for visionOS. With the 24.8 release, we've taken this further by expanding the. Now, you can leverage all the authentication types supported by SAP Mobile Services, ensuring seamless and secure logins for your users. This release also brings advanced security features such as App Attestation, and Clipboard restriction. Additionally, developers can now build visionOS apps that can run offline.

 

Assistant Improvements

With the latest release of SAP BTP SDK for iOS, the SAP BTP SDK Assistant has been enhanced to support the creation of applications using IAS (Identity Authentication Service) security settings. Previously, this option was available only on SAP Mobile Services. Now, you can directly configure IAS security settings in the Assistant, alongside the existing support for XSUAA.

The Assistant now fully supports Complex OData Types in the scaffolding applications it generates. Whether you're using the generated app as a reference or a starting point, it will handle OData services with complex types, simplifying the development of UIs based on complex data structures.

 

Foundation Enhancements

In this release, the Foundation framework has been enhanced to support Apple’s bytes API. This powerful addition enables you to retrieve the contents of a URL request as an asynchronous sequence of bytes. With this capability, you can now stream response data in real-time without needing to wait for the entire response to be fully downloaded.

Additionally, the latest version of the SDK introduces Secure Store APIs, allowing you to securely store sensitive information such as authentication tokens. This feature will accelerate your watchOS development, allowing you to store authentication context between your phone app and watch extension.

 

OData

By upgrading to the 24.8.0's OData Library, you can start using the $search & $filter OData queries for both online and offline apps. The offline apps are also capable of using OData open type and a single entity type for containment of multuple parent entity sets.

 

SAP Fiori

The 24.8 version includes several additions and enhancements to SAP Fiori & SAP Fiori Flows. You can now integrate third-party scanning solutions into the scanning flow. By introducing a list of scanning options, users can select from multiple available scanning solutions, with the added ability to search through these options.

The search bar has been improved to better handle various states involved in speech recognition, offering a more intuitive and seamless user experience when performing voice-based searches.

Additionally, starting with this release, you can register for a more detailed session that will cover all the new capabilities in depth. To join, register for the SAP Fiori for Android & iOS Release Rollout here.

 

Raise A Request

If you have feature ideas that the SDK should support, please raise an improvement request here: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/2279.

 

Feature Summary

This blog highlights some of the features released with the new version of SAP BTP SDK for iOS – 24.8.0. For details, please check the important links section.

  • visionOS Development Enhancements
  • IAS & Complex OData Types in Assistant Generated Apps
  • Stream Data from URLs using the Foundation Framework
  • Enhanced and New Fiori Horizon Controls like enabling 3rd party scanners

 

Important Links

  • SAP BTP SDK for iOS 24.8 Download (Software Center)
  • Documentation
  • API Reference

 

Acknowledgement

A huge kudos to the spectacular team that worked tirelessly, right from conceptualizing the improvements, to defining, designing, building, and eventually delivering them.

entry.title: SAP BTP SDK for Android 24.8.0 is now available
entry.content:
Release announcement: SAP BTP SDK for Android 24.8.0 can now be downloaded

The new version of SAP BTP SDK for Android – version 24.8.0 is now available.

You can download the latest version of the SDK either from the Software Center or the downloads section on develeopers.sap.com

The SAP BTP SDK for Android team brings some exciting new features to you with this new release.

In this blog, we will highlight some of the cool features that come with the new upgrade. To get a detailed overview of all the features released, please visit our user documentation and our API reference documentation.

 

Highlights

 

Wizard

With the 24.8 release, the SAP BTP SDK for Android Wizard now supports Android Studio Koala and Android Studio Jellyfish.

Additionally, the Wizard now allows the creation of applications using IAS (Identity Authentication Service) security settings, a feature previously available only on SAP Mobile Services. Developers can now select IAS security settings directly within the Wizard, alongside the existing support for XSUAA.

 

OData

By upgrading to the 24.8.0's OData Library, you can start using the $search & $filter OData queries for both online and offline apps. The offline apps are also capable of using OData open type and a single entity type for containment of multuple parent entity sets.

 

SAP Fiori

Jetpack Compose Controls

By using the 24.8 version, you can now use a Floating Action Button (FAB) using Jetpack Compose. Additionally, you can specify limits and display them in the attachment control.

XML-View Based Controls

You can now integrate third-party scanning solutions into the view based scanning control. Users can select from multiple available scanning solutions, with the added ability to search through these options. Similar to Jetpack Compose, you can define and display limits in the attachment control. You can also add helper text for FilterChip, Choice, DateTimePicker and DurationPicker form cell controls.

SAP Fiori Flows

The SDK now allows customization of the information presented during the sign-in flow. This is particularly useful in multi-user scenarios, where you might want to display customized fields, such as first names, instead of the default IDP-provided username.

SAP Fiori Rollout Session

Additionally, starting with this release, you can register for a more detailed session that will cover all the new capabilities in depth. To join, register for the SAP Fiori for Android & iOS Release Rollout here.

 

Raise A Request

If you have feature ideas that the SDK should support, please raise an improvement request here: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/2279.

 

Feature Summary

This blog highlights some of the features released with the new version of SAP BTP SDK for Android – 24.8.0. For details, please check the important links section.

  • Support for Android Studio Koala and Jellyfish
  • Use IAS Security Setting in Wizard Generated Application
  • Enhancements to OData Library
  • New Fiori Horizon Controls on Jetpack Compose and XML based Views

 

Important Links

  • SAP BTP SDK for Android 24.8.0 (Software Center)
  • Documentation
  • API Reference

 

Acknowledgement
A huge kudos to the spectacular team that worked tirelessly, right from conceptualizing the improvements, to defining, designing, building, and eventually delivering them.

entry.title: SAP Fiori development newsletter September 2024 (issue #30)
entry.content:
September marks the start of autumn, which means back to school for our kids and conference season for us. Both are focused on learning new things, which is so important in our industry where technology changes so quickly.

Some events to look for include Devtoberfest, SAP TechEd, and the next SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Heidelberg.

If you want to meet with fellow developers and the SAP engineers who build our professional development tools, I encourage you to join our monthly SAP Fiori elements and SAP Fiori tools roundtable group. Our next meeting is Wednesday, October 9, 2024. You can also email me if you would like an invitation.

If you prefer to receive this in your inbox, please subscribe to the SAP Fiori development newsletter.

Development News

How to create an SAP Fiori elements application from within ADT

This integration helps bridge the gaps between the different development environments that are currently required to build an SAP Fiori elements app and deploy it on an ABAP stack.
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-create-a-sap-fiori-elements-application-from-within-adt/ba-p/13797232

Side Effects in SAP Fiori elements apps: triggering actions and updating data in your unmanaged RAP application Side Effects refer to system behaviors where a user’s action, such as modifying a field or performing a specific task, triggers changes in other parts of the user interface. For instance, updating a field might automatically adjust related fields or data sections. These changes are crucial for maintaining data consistency and ensuring that the user interface reflects the latest information.
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/side-effects-in-fiori-triggering-actions-and-updating-data-in-your/ba-p/13851571

How to mass upload from a spreadsheet file into an SAP Fiori app in SAP BTP ABAP Environment

All SAP Fiori elements apps provide a download to Excel as a standard feature. However, only a few apps support uploading from a spreadsheet. In this series, you will see how to upload records from a spreadsheet file.
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-mass-upload-from-a-spreadsheet-file-into-an-sap-fiori-app-in-sap-btp/ba-p/13785946 (part 1)
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-mass-upload-from-a-spreadsheet-file-into-an-sap-fiori-app-in-sap-btp/ba-p/13800792 (part 2)

Smart and flexible: sap.ui.mdc library is released for productive use in SAPUI5 1.124

sap.ui.mdc helps to build enterprise-ready smart controls or applications, no matter which back-end technology and data protocol you are using. It offers a flexible and comprehensive reuse approach with implementable delegates for the sap.ui.mdc controls. Using specific protocols like FHIR or even a plain REST service is now possible without having to start from scratch.
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/smart-and-flexible-sap-ui-mdc-released-for-productive-use/ba-p/13793439

SAP BTP ABAP environment – release 2408

A summary of updates to ADT, CDS, RAP, and other innovations for ABAP developers.
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-btp-abap-environment-release-2408/ba-p/13801154

Events

Devtoberfest starts September 23

Devtoberfest aims to capture the experience of live pre-conference events, like SAP InnoJam of years’ past. It officially kicks off the SAP TechEd season. Devtoberfest takes some of the best aspects of the traditional hackathon type event, such as learning by building something, but opens the experience to the entire community and spreads across multiple weeks to better accommodate everyone’s busy schedules.
https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest-blog-posts/devtoberfest-2024-welcome/ba-p/9358

SAP TechEd runs October 8-9

User experience and app dev sessions at SAP TechEd 2024

For 2024, SAP TechEd is virtual once again, with lots of great sessions grouped into six content tracks. If you prefer in-person learning, SAP TechEd on Tour consists of companion events around the world in collaboration with regional user groups (ASUG, SAUG, UKISUG, and DSAG).

Here are some sessions on UX and application development.
https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/user-experience-and-app-dev-sessions-at-sap-teched-2024-virtual/ba-p/13864322

SAPUI5 at SAP TechEd: tailor your UI with SAPUI5 in SAP Build Code

Discover the latest on SAPUI5 development in SAP Build Code. Learn how to realize clean core extensibility with new capabilities and how you can future-proof your app with new tools and best practices. Understand why SAP Web Components are strategic and how you can benefit from using them.
See AD101 in the session catalog

Full TechEd session catalog

Content tracks – we recommend you check the application development and automation tracks

SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Heidelberg on November 19

After our successful events in Dublin and Chicago, we are bringing SAP Fiori Innovation Day back to Europe. The next one will be in Heidelberg on November 19. By attending this event, you will learn:

  • SAP’s UX strategy and how it is evolving
  • Recent UX innovations that fulfill the promise of the intelligent, collaborative enterprise
  • Making SAP Fiori work for you – finding the apps you need and adapting them to your business requirements
  • Building apps using the latest tools and technologies – the same ones SAP uses to build apps for SAP S/4HANA
  • How to move your organization forward, including how to take advantage of AI for developing applications

Register today:
https://events.sap.com/eur/sap-fiori-innovation-day-heidelberg-2024/en/home

Summary of SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Chicago

If you want to gain a sense of what we do in SAP Fiori Innovation Day and what you might learn, you can read the summary of our event in Chicago.
https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/sap-fiori-innovation-day-in-chicago-sap-fiori-fundamentals-ux/ba-p/13797787

Tutorials

openSAP content has moved to the learning.sap.com platform

Developing and Extending SAP Fiori Elements Apps
https://learning.sap.com/courses/developing-and-extending-sap-fiori-elements-apps

Developing an SAP Fiori Elements App Based on a CAP OData V4 Service
https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/developing-an-sap-fiori-elements-app-based-on-a-cap-odata-v4-service

Getting Started with Creating an SAP Fiori Elements App Based on an OData V4 RAP Service

This new learning journey guides you through building SAP Fiori elements applications from scratch using a RAP OData V4 service.
https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/getting-started-with-creating-an-sap-fiori-elements-app-based-on-an-odata-v4-rap-service

Adapting the UI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Using SAP Screen Personas

https://learning.sap.com/courses/adapting-the-ui-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-using-sap-screen-personas

Tutorial: Key User Adaptation for SAPUI5 applications

To extend SAPUI5 applications, SAPUI5 Flexibility offers capabilities not only for developers, but also for key users and end users using simple visual / WYSIWYG tools.
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/new-tutorial-mission-key-user-adaptation-for-sapui5-applications/ba-p/13639694

Tutorial: SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects for SAP S/4HANA Cloud

SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects in SAP Business Application Studio are not new. What is new, is the support for SAP S/HANA Cloud. This enables developers to enhance SAPUI5 applications in SAP S/4HANA Cloud with features that were previously only for on-premise customers. See this blog post linking to an extensive, end-to-end tutorial:
https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/extending-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-using-sapui5-adaptation-projects/ba-p/13607813

Back issues from the past year

SAP Fiori development newsletter July 2024 (issue #29)

SAP FIori development newsletter #28 (May 2024)

SAP Fiori development newsletter #27 (March 2024)

SAP Fiori development newsletter #26 (January 2024)

SAP Fiori development newsletter #25 (November 2023)

entry.title: SAP Developer News, September 19th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/09/19/sap-developer-news-september-19th-2024/

DESCRIPTION

Devtoberfest Launch

  • Devtoberfest Community Home: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/gh-p/Devtoberfest
  • Devtoberfest Events Calendar: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/eb-p/devtoberfest-events?calendar=true
  • Devtoberfest Gameboard: https://devrel-tools-prod-scn-badges-srv.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/devtoberfestContest/scnId.Here
  • Devtoberfest Playlist on YouTube: https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQDHz97qsNTNAE2jnUKj8X5d&si=R26dQIns-1e97fU0

SAP Cloud Application Programming Model August 2024 Release

  • Release notes https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/releases/aug24

Devtoberfest Crossword Puzzle

  • https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/devtoberfest2024-sap-crossword.html

Transporting content in SAP Build Apps

  •  https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-builders-blog-posts/transporting-content-in-sap-build-apps-sap-cloud-transport-management/ba-p/13737478

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro
0:10 Devtoberfest Launch
2:09 SAP Cloud Application Programming Model August 2024 Release
7:56 Devtoberfest Puzzle
9:06 Transporting content in SAP Build Apps

Transcript

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for September 19th, 2024.

[Sheena] Hello SAP community. The much-awaited Detoberfest 2024 is here, running from September 23rd to October 25th. Devtoberfest helps to prepare for SAP TechEd and offers a mix of educational sessions, hands-on developer tools, and fun activities like Petoberfest, Bugtoberfest, and Spooktoberfest. It is a point-based contest, and the Devtoberfest animated contest game board is already live. You can earn points by attending sessions, completing developer tutorials linked to weekly topics or engaging in activities that benefit the SAP Developer Community.

The grand prize is a retreat at an SAP TechEd Roadshow stop in 2025. Exciting, right? To start participating, join the Devtoberfest group after reviewing the legal terms and conditions posted there.

We will kickstart Devtoberfest on Monday, September 23rd, which is the ABAP/CAP Day. Week 1, we are bringing to you many exciting sessions in ABAP, mainly on clean core extensibility, building partner products on SAP BTP, major updates in embedded analytics, and data integration in ABAP Cloud with SQL services and CDS external entities. Week 1 also covers an exciting session on CAP, it's time to TypeScript (CAP edition).

On tooling Tuesdays, we'll focus on SAP Build Process Automation, Build Code and Build Apps.

Integration Wednesdays will have sessions on FlashPy, CPI Helper, and CPI Lint.

MAD Thursdays will focus on machine learning, AI, and data with sessions on SAP Generative AI Hub, SAP Datasphere, etc.

Frontend Fridays will have sessions on UI5 Freestyle and SAP Fiori tools.

Don't forget to participate in the fun Friday event Petoberfest by posting pictures with your pets at your workstation. So join us in this fun ride of learning and enjoy. Good morning.

[DJ] The latest release of CAP August of 2024, in fact, is out. And with it, there are improvements and fixes to the latest major release 8, which came out earlier this year, in the form of CAP Node.js 8.2.1 and CAP Java 3.2.0. Let me take this opportunity on my walk this morning to go through some of those highlights.

So first up, when the latest major release of CAP came out earlier this year, the new database services went GA, including the database service for SAP HANA. One of the benefits of this new database service for the Node.js runtime is that HANA native associations were no longer required to represent associations at the CDS level. This was great, mostly because it brought longer deploy times when we used SAP HANA Native Associations, plus there was the chance of indirect dependencies also being created. And if you're wondering about the Java runtime, the Java runtime hasn't used native SAP HANA Associations for a while. Anyway, at the time, the release notes advised us to switch off the HANA native associations using configuration. With this August 2024 release, that switch is now automatic. When you start a new project and do, for example, CDS add HANA, you get a switch in the configuration that turns these native associations off by default.

In the area of the CDS language and compiler, you can now use CDS deploy dash dash script to generate a SQL script as a starting point or manual schema migration. Note that in contrast to the existing dash dash dry option, it accepts and produces DDL statements that could lead to data loss. So are A, not supported for automatic schema migration and B, contains relevant warnings in the right places.

In the Node.js runtime, there are also some important changes. One of which is that linked definitions, the class that is used to represent internally structures of services and more, now no longer have the convenience methods such as for each map filter and so on that you would normally expect to have to process the linked definitions as arrays. Because basically linked definitions are not arrays, they're more than that. So these methods have been removed. In my opinion, this is great because it reminds us that we have to treat the data as it is. And so one thing to do is maybe convert the data to arrays using the rest parameter inside, for example, an array, literal array constructor.

Another improvement is that you can now programmatically, i.e. dynamically at runtime, control and activate the CDS debug output at runtime. This means you can turn on debug for cat modules as you go.

In the Java runtime, there's now beta level support for recursive hierarchies with Odata v4 in SAP HANA. Recursive hierarchy is an experimental term in the OData hierarchy vocabulary and is used, for example, with SAP Fiori tree tables. Treetables. You can also now define the name of the Java interfaces generated for CDS entities and structured types using a new annotation. In the multi-tenancy area, activating extensions using the SAP extensibility service can now be done asynchronously. You achieve this by sending a prefer header in the HTTP request with the value of respond async. This in my opinion is a really neat way of coordinating this using the RFC 7240 standard authored by an old acquaintance of mine.

Well I'm almost home but there's just time to tell you about improvements in the tools area. So first for all you IntelliJ users out there the SAP CDS language support plugin is now available on the JetBrains marketplace which means it's directly installable now. Great! For all you TypeScript users there's a new CLI command "CDS TSX", which in contrast to the existing CDS TS command uses a different faster engine. This feature is in beta still at the moment.

So I'm back home. There's one more thing to tell you about and that is if you want to use the SAP Cloud Logging Service instead of the SAP Application Logging Service, you can do that. And there's an option to use telemetry as well, including an option to use Dynatrace. What's not to like? Anyway, that's it for this August 2024 release. See See you next time.

[Shrini and Daniel] Hello, everybody. I'm at Tbilisi, Georgia with Daniel at the EPUB office doing SAP Build CodeJam. It's really great to be here. Yeah, it's been a lot of fun and because it's only a couple days before Devtoberfest, we wanted to tell you something about the contest. So the contest you can earn enough points to get a trip to TechEd perhaps next year and one of the ways to get points is by doing the traditional SAP crossword. So there are topics about SAP Build and JavaScript and even a reference to Analytics Cloud and it would help to know something about Sabrina Carpenter. Can anybody do the Cross Word? Yes, definitely. Of course, it helps to know a little bit about SAP. There are 33 questions. Fairly easy, a few tougher ones, but I think everybody can do it, and it might even be worth a couple thousand points. And it's already published, so even before the Devtoberfest starts, you can accumulate points. So that's pretty good. That's good to know. Yeah. Good luck to everybody.

[Rekha] Hello, everybody. We all know the importance of having development, test, and productive environments during the application development. SAP has recently introduced project versioning for SAP Build apps, web, and mobile projects. This allows you to transport the released project versions between development, testing, and productive subaccounts on SAP BTP. A transport setup of Cloud Transport Management Service, CTMS, Content Agent Service, CAS, and BTP destinations are required to start transporting SAP Build Apps projects between the three tiered dev test prod landscapes. There are four types of subaccounts or tenants involved in the scenario. A source subaccount, this is where the actual development takes place. A target, under this we have two subaccounts, testing, and production, and a central sub-account to administer the content transport between the source and target sub-accounts. In order to enable the transport, you will have to enable the Cloud Foundry, subscribe to SAP Build apps and content agent services, create an instance of content agent, and generate a service key on the source and target sub-accounts.

On the central sub-account, you need to subscribe to Cloud Transport Management service, create an instance of Cloud Transport Management, and generate a service key. The MBS role collection has to be enabled in BTP cockpit to authorize the users for performing the transport activities. SAP Burn Lobby configuration has to be done in the control tower in order to set the sub-account type to decide if it's a dev, test, or prod subaccount. Then BTP destinations has to be set from source to central subaccount, and then from central to target, that is the test and prod subaccounts. Finally, set up the transport landscape using the Cloud Transport Management user interface. For more details on this topic, do refer to this interesting blog post by SAP Build expert Mari Heary. Link is provided in the description box. Enjoy learning and thank you.

entry.title: AD104 - Next steps
entry.content:
In our Teched session AD104 you have learned about how to build CAP applications using SAP Build Code. And how to enrich this application with prebuilt functionality and service integrations by utilizing CAP Plugins.

After entering into the development environment from the SAP Build Code Lobby, we used generative AI by providing it with a textual description of our use case. Joule AI used this input and generated very useful artefacts for us:

  • A complete CDS data model
  • A CDS service definition
  • Fiori Elements UI annotations
  • Sample data

If you think of a typical 3 tier architecture everything is covered. From the CDS data model the CAP framework automatically creates the database statements covering the database layer. The CAP application itself is the server and with our CDS service definition we have defined the behavior of the server and the endpoints it provides (by default via Odata v4). And the UI annotations provide the information to render a Fiori Elements User Interface that matches our model.

So one simple command created a complete full stack application, that we can start up an try out.

And with the sample data (which even fits the business context!) we can try out the application with meaningful test data.

This is a great starting point - and we can start to enhance the application now with additional functionalities and our business logic.

One common requirement across applications is uploading attachments. On our incidents sample app, it would be very helpful to upload supporting documents. Previously, that would have been a challenge connected with a ton of custom code, maintenance and so on. Maybe a thousand lines of code to access low level APIs of an attachment service, where you need to take care of everything. Plus the additional logic - what is the maximum attachment size? How to malware scan it? How to implement the UI to upload and download attachments.

Image instead a single dependency to a reusable package and two lines of code - that includes all of the above already.

That's the power of CAP Plugins and the story behind "Calesi" - CAP level service interfaces.

___

If you want to try out this functionality, the Joule functionalities are now available in SAP Build Code on a trial account.

The instructions for this can be found here:

https://developers.sap.com/mission.sap-build-code-test-drive.html

___

For a list of officially supported CAP plugins, check out the corresponding page in our documentation:

https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/plugins/

For node.js there are two github organizations where all the plugins can be found:

cap-js : https://github.com/search?q=topic%3Aplugin+org%3Acap-js&type=Repositories

cap-js-community: https://github.com/cap-js-community

For a Devtoberfest session, where you can follow along hands on and learn how to add additional plugins and a custom handler to a Joule generated app, see https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4o2Swj89zxU 

___

Any more questions, feedback and so on - feel free to leave a comment!

Thank you for reading 😃

entry.title: Devtoberfest 2024 - Tooling (SAP Build) Sessions
entry.content:
Today is the first day of Devoberfest!!

Make sure you partake of the:

  • Contest -- grand prize is a trip to an SAP TechEd event with a lot of extras; and even though it's several years' old I still love the gameboard
  • Petoberfest – post a picture of your pet 🐢
  • Sessions – find all the sessions in the event calendar 
  • 👉 The annual SAP Crossword Puzzle – if you have the skill, you can earn 1800 points!

Devtoberfest 2024 sessions

Here's a quick rundown of the SAP Build "Tooling Tuesday" sessions, which touch on the following topics:

  • SAP Build Apps
  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Build Work Zone
  • SAP Build Code
  • MDK
  • S/4HANA + extensions
  • Integration Suite, including event mesh
  • CAP
  • AI Core
  • GenAI + Joule
  • SAP Cloud Transport Management

That's just one day of Devtoberfest!! In each session, you'll have a chance to ask questions directly to the presenter, who will answer them live or in the chat.

September 24

  • 🟣 Transform Data with Scripting in SAP Build Process Automation

  • 🟣 Develop a Full-Stack CAP Application with SAP Build Code

  • 🟣 Develop with SAP Build Apps Like a Pro

  • 🟣 Real-World S/4HANA Invoice Approval Process with SAP Build Process Automation

October 1

  • 🟣 SAP Build Apps 2049: Soon with UI5 and GenAI?

  • 🟣 Enable Work Zone to Consume Business Solutions from Other BTP Subaccounts (content federation)

  • 🟣 Cross-Platform Mobile App Development with SAP Build Code and the MDK

  • 🟣 Putting It All Together: S/4HANA Retail Pricing App Using SAP Build, AI, Event Mesh and More

October 15

  • 🟣 S/4HANA Extensions: Start Fast with the New Extensibility Wizard

  • 🟣 Guided Experiences with SAP Build Process Automation and Work Zone

  • 🟣 Develop Even Faster (!) with SAP Build Apps Templates

  • 🟣 Boost Development with GenAI in SAP Build Process Automation

October 22

  • 🟣 Chatbot Development Made Easy with SAP AI Core and Build Apps

  • 🟣 Set Up Rules for Who Can Create and Transport SAP Build Projects

  • 🟣 Leveraging Decisions and Business Rules in SAP Build Process Automation

  • 🟣 Extend Joule with Your Own Skills

Learn a lot about SAP development tools, have a lot of fun – and maybe we'll see you at an SAP TechEd event next year 😸

entry.title: SAP MDK integration with SAP AI Core services: Part 6 – Work Order and Operation Recording
entry.content:
In this series of blogs, I will showcase how to integrate SAP AI Core services into SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) to develop mobile applications with AI capabilities.

  • Part 1: SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) integration with SAP AI Core services - Setup
  • Part 2: SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) integration with SAP AI Core services - Business Use Cases
  • Part 3: SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) integration with SAP AI Core services - Measurement Readings
  • Part 4: SAP MDK integration with SAP AI Core services - Anomaly Detection and Maintenance Guidelines
  • Part 5: SAP MDK integration with SAP AI Core services - Retrieval Augmented Generation
  • Part 6: SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) integration with SAP AI Core services - Work Order and Operation Recording (Current Blog)

In the previous blog, we showcased how to setup the SAP HANA Cloud vector engine and understand Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG). In this blog, we will explore how to record orders and operations using voice and free text input.

SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) integration with SAP AI Core services - Work Order and Operation Recording

 First, we will introduce how RAG is used in our use case. RAG is used during AI calls to generate specific form cells on the operation details page. For example, a water pump may have different details compared to a laptop. RAG ensures that the AI generates the appropriate operation details. Currently, the knowledge base, which we introduced in a preview blog, is limited to a few equipment types (e.g., water pumps, electricity meters) with very limited content. In a real case, there will be more equipment types, each having a large volume of information. In that case, we will benefit from the fast similarity searching capability of the SAP HANA Vector Database. In our blog, we will showcase a very simple example with the equipment schema as the only useful knowledge.

We will use a JavaScript function to retrieve the equipment schema from an instance of the HANA Vector Database. This schema includes both the common schema and specific schema. Remember the common schema and specific schema of the water pump as part of our domain specific knowledge, that we populated into the SAP HANA Cloud vector database in the previous blog? Let’s refresh our memory below.

 

Common Schema
[
  {"Title": "taskTitle", "Description": "Operation Title", "Type": "Text"},
  {"Title": "shortDescription", "Description": "Operation short description", "Type": "Text"},
  {"Title": "description", "Description": "Detailed operation description", "Type": "Text"},
  {"Title": "priority", "Description": "priority (high, medium, low)", "Type": "Text"},
]

Specific Schema for SAPPump:
[
  {"Title": "yearOfInstallation", "Description": "Year of installation", "Type": "Text"},
  {"Title": "length", "Description": "Length of the water pump in meters", "Type": "Text"}
]

 

Now we will use the JavaScript function below to retrieve the water pump equipment schema.

 

async function getEquipmentSchema(context, equipmentName) {
  const functionName = 'getRagResponse';
  const serviceName = '/MDKDevApp/Services/CAPVectorEngine.service';
  const parameters = {
    "value": `In JSON, Get the Common schema and the specific schema of the equipment ${equipmentName}. The common schema cannot be empty. The title of each property must be in camel cases. In camel case, you start a name with a small letter. If the name has multiple words, the later words will start with a capital letter`
  };
  let oFunction = { Name: functionName, Parameters: parameters };
  try {
    const response = await context.callFunction(serviceName, oFunction);
    const responseObj = JSON.parse(response);
    return responseObj.completion.content;
  } catch (error) {
    console.log("Error:", error.message);
  }
  return Promise.resolve("");
}

 

The above code snippet uses the MDK Client API's `callFunction` method to call the OData function `getRagResponse` on the `CAPVectorEngine` OData service. It retrieves the common schema and the specific schema of the equipment in JSON format.

The returned result of equipment schema is as below

 

Equipment schema: {
    "commonSchema": {
      "taskTitle": "Text",
      "shortDescription": "Text",
      "description": "Text",
      "priority": "Text"
    },
    "SAPPump": {
      "yearOfInstallation": "Text",
      "length": "Text"
    }
  }

 

Once the equipment schema is fetched, we will use it to generate a form cell

 

// Generate form cells
    console.log("Preparing form cells...")
    spinnerID = context.showActivityIndicator('Preparing forms...');
    const formCellSchema = await generateFormCells(context, equipmentSchema);
    console.log("Form cells schema:", formCellSchema);
    console.log(`Form cells prepared!`);
    context.dismissActivityIndicator();

 

Here, we will call the `generateFormCells` function with the equipment schema fetched above as the input parameter.

 

async function generateFormCells(context, schema) {
  const method = "POST";
  const path = "/chat/completions?api-version=2024-02-01";
  const body = {
    messages: [
      {
        role: "user",
        content: `Generate form filling cells based on the schema provided as mentioned. ${schema}`
      }
    ],
    max_tokens: 256,
    temperature: 0,
    frequency_penalty: 0,
    presence_penalty: 0,
    functions: [{ name: "format_response", parameters: formCellSchema }],
    function_call: { name: "format_response" },
  };
  const headers = {
    "content-type": "application/json",
    "AI-Resource-Group": "default"
  };
  const service = "/MDKDevApp/Services/AzureOpenAI.service";
  return context.executeAction({
    "Name": "/MDKDevApp/Actions/SendRequest.action",
    "Properties": {
      "ShowActivityIndicator": false,
      "Target": {
        "Path": path,
        "RequestProperties": {
          "Method": method,
          "Headers": headers,
          "Body": JSON.stringify(body)
        },
        "Service": service
      }
    }
  }).then(response => {
    const resultsObj = JSON.parse(response.data.choices[0].message.function_call.arguments);
    return resultsObj;
  });
}

 

Again, we will send a POST request to the SAP AI Core service via the AzureOpenAI destination, using the same AI Core service deployment as in previous blogs for anomaly detection and meter reading. The difference is that this time we will not upload an image; instead, we will use the equipment schema we obtained in the last step as the content.

 

        content: `Generate form filling cells based on the schema provided as mentioned. ${schema}`

 

We also use an LLM function call to specify the structured output we want from the AI service. Please note that how to define an LLM function call may vary between different LLMs. This example is based on OpenAI GPT-4. For details, refer to OpenAI's documentation (https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/function-calling ). If you are using another LLM, consult the respective vendor's documentation to understand how to make function calls or, more generally, how to connect LLMs to external tools.

 

functions: [{ name: "format_response", parameters: formCellSchema }],
    function_call: { name: "format_response" },

 

For our example, we make a simple function call to format the response into structured data by passing the `formCellSchema` below as an input parameter.

 

const formCellSchema = {
  "type": "object",
  "properties": {
    "commonSchema": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Refers to each item in the common schema array",
      "items": {
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
          "title": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Title of the property. Must be in camel case and match exactly as defined in schema"
          },
          "value": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Value of the property. Must be an empty string if it takes in a string/number. Must be false if it takes in a boolean. Otherwise, it must be an empty string"
          },
          "type": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "If the schema object is a value of string or number, it will be 'Control.Type.FormCell.SimpleProperty'. If it is a boolean it will be 'Control.Type.FormCell.Switch'. Otherwise, it is a 'Control.Type.FormCell.SegmentedControl'"
          }
        },
        "required": ["title", "value", "type"]
      }
    },
    "specificSchema": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Refers to each item in the specific schema array",
      "items": {
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
          "title": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Title of the property. Must be in camel case and match exactly as defined in schema"
          },
          "value": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Value of the property. Must be an empty string if it takes in a string/number. Must be false if it takes in a boolean. Otherwise, it must be an empty string"
          },
          "type": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "If the schema object is a value of string or number, it will be 'Control.Type.FormCell.SimpleProperty'. If it is a boolean it will be 'Control.Type.FormCell.Switch'. Otherwise, it is a 'Control.Type.FormCell.SegmentedControl'"
          }
        },
        "required": ["title", "value", "type"]
      }
    }
  },
  "required": ["commonSchema", "specificSchema"]
};

 

The formCellSchema object defines the structure of a schema used to generate form cells. It consists of two main parts: commonSchema and specificSchema. Both are arrays of objects, each describing properties of form cells. One is commonSchema, which corresponds to the commonSchema of EquipmentSchema. The other is specificSchema, which corresponds to SAPPump of EquipmentSchema. EquipmentSchema  is returned by getRagResponse from HANA vector database in previous step. With the above code, MDK metadata of two form cells will be generated by the AI core service. For our example, it will be something like below:

 

Form cells schema: {
    "specificSchema": [
      {
        "title": "yearOfInstallation",
        "value": "",
        "type": "Control.Type.FormCell.SimpleProperty"
      },
      {
        "title": "length",
        "value": "",
        "type": "Control.Type.FormCell.SimpleProperty"
      }
    ],
    "commonSchema": [
      {
        "title": "taskTitle",
        "value": "",
        "type": "Control.Type.FormCell.SimpleProperty"
      },
      {
        "title": "shortDescription",
        "value": "",
        "type": "Control.Type.FormCell.SimpleProperty"
      },
      {
        "title": "description",
        "value": "",
        "type": "Control.Type.FormCell.SimpleProperty"
      },
      {
        "title": "priority",
        "value": "",
        "type": "Control.Type.FormCell.SimpleProperty"
      }
    ]
  }

 

Now we will save above metadata to a variable to use later.

 

    const pageProxy = context.evaluateTargetPathForAPI("#Page:MDKGenAIPage");
    const cd = pageProxy.getClientData();
    if (!cd) { console.log("Empty"); return; }
    if (!cd.OperationsData) {
      cd.OperationsData = [];
}

const schema = [...formCellSchema.commonSchema, ...formCellSchema.specificSchema]
task.schema = JSON.stringify(schema);
cd.OperationsData = [...cd.OperationsData, ...tasks];

 

`PageProxy` is a developer-facing interface that provides access to a page. It is passed to rules to enable application-specific customizations. Here, we use the `PageProxy` of the current MDK page `MDKGenAIPage` to store the above metadata.

Next, we will use above metadata to create a MDK page. Instead of creating page with static page metadata, we will use below Javascript rules to dynamically create page with above metadata.

 

import ParseKey from "./ParseKey";

function getFormCellSchema(context) {
  try {
    const binding = context.binding;
    const schema = JSON.parse(binding.OperationData?.schema);
    const results = [];
    schema.forEach(formcell => {
      let value = formcell.value;
      for (const key in binding.OperationData) {
        if (binding.OperationData.hasOwnProperty(key) && formcell.title === key) {
          value = binding.OperationData[key];
        }
      }
      results.push({
        "_Type": formcell.type,
        "Caption": ParseKey(formcell.title),
        "Value": value,
        "_Name": formcell.title,
      })
    })
    return results;
  } catch (e) {
    console.log(e)
  }
}

export default function NavToOperationEdit(context) {
  try {
    const pageProxy = context.getPageProxy();
    const schema = getFormCellSchema(context);

    const pageMetataObj = {
      "Caption": "Operation Detail Edit",
      "ActionBar": {
        "Items": [
          {
            "Position": "right",
            "SystemItem": "sparkles",
            "Text": "Edit",
            "OnPress": "/MDKDevApp/Rules/ImageAnalysis/NavToRecordPage.js"
          },
          {
            "Position": "right",
            "Text": "Confirm",
            "OnPress": "/MDKDevApp/Rules/ImageAnalysis/SaveData.js"
          }
        ]
      },
      "Controls": [
        {
          "Sections": [
            {
              "_Type": "Section.Type.FormCell",
              "_Name": "FormCellSection",
              "Controls": schema
            }
          ],
          "_Name": "SectionedTable",
          "_Type": "Control.Type.SectionedTable"
        }
      ],
      "_Name": "OperationEdit",
      "_Type": "Page"
    }

    const pageMetadata = JSON.stringify(pageMetataObj);

    return pageProxy.executeAction({
      "Name": "/MDKDevApp/Actions/NavToPage.action",
      "Properties": {
        "PageToOpen": "/MDKDevApp/Pages/Empty.page",
        "PageMetadata": pageMetadata
      }
    });
  } catch (e) {
    console.log(e)
  }
}

 

First, the getFormCellSchema function extracts the form cell schema from the PageProxy. Next, we will define the page metadata object pageMetadataObj. You may notice that in the FormCellSection, the value of Controls is actually the form cell schema from the PageProxy. Lastly, the metadata object pageMetadataObj will be stringified and used to replace the content of an empty page. In this way, a page like the screenshot below will be created dynamically.

Now, we are going to fill out the operation detail form to create a new work order for equipment maintenance. Instead of manually entering the required information, such as the year of installation and the length of the water pipe, we implement hands-free input using voice input from a mobile device. First, we use the NativeScript speech-recognition plugin to convert voice input to text. Simply add nativescript-speech-recognition to MDKProject.json:

 

{
  "AppName": "MDK GenAI",
  "AppVersion": "1.0.0",
  "BundleID": "com.sap.mdk.genai",
  "Externals": ["nativescript-speech-recognition"],
  "NSPlugins": ["nativescript-speech-recognition"],
  "UrlScheme": "mdkclient"
}

Then, we can use this nativescript plugin in a Javascript rule as below
export default async function VoiceInput(context) {
  let userInput = "";

  if (context.nativescript.platformModule.isIOS || context.nativescript.platformModule.isAndroid) {
    // require the plugin
    let SpeechRecognition = require("nativescript-speech-recognition").SpeechRecognition;

    // instantiate the plugin
    let speechRecognition = new SpeechRecognition();
    let pageProxy;
    let shouldUpdateNote = false;
    try {
      pageProxy = context.evaluateTargetPathForAPI("#Page:SpeechAnalysisUseCasePage");
    } catch (err) {
      pageProxy = context.evaluateTargetPathForAPI("#Page:OperationDetail");
      shouldUpdateNote = true;
    }

    // save speech recognition object to stop listening 
    const cd = pageProxy.getClientData();
    cd.SpeechRecognitionObj = speechRecognition;

    try {
      const available = await speechRecognition.available();
      if (available) {
        cd.isListening = true;
        cd.recordingRestarted = true;
        context.getParent().redraw();
        const transcription = await speechRecognition.startListening(
          {
            // set to true to get results back continuously
            returnPartialResults: true,
            // this callback will be invoked repeatedly during recognition
            onResult: (transcription) => {
              UpdateProgress(context, transcription.text, shouldUpdateNote, cd);
              userInput = transcription.text; // assign the result 
              // console.log(`User finished?: ${transcription.finished}`);
              if (transcription.finished) {
                DismissProgress(context, shouldUpdateNote);
                return userInput;
              }
            },
            onError: (error) => {
            }
          }
        );

        const pageProxy = context.getPageProxy();
        const sectionProxy = pageProxy.evaluateTargetPathForAPI("#Section:FormCellSection");
        const controlProxy = sectionProxy.getControl('NoteFormCell');
        controlProxy.setEditable(false);
      }
    } catch (error) {
      console.log(`Error: ${error}`);
    }
    //for POC wait for 10 seconds to finish speech recognition
    await sleep(10000).then(() => {
      return Promise.resolve();
    });
    console.log('The voice input has finished!');
    // DismissProgress(clientAPI);
    return userInput;
  } else {
    // voice input not support on web, use default input for now
    return userInput;
  }
}

 

With the above code snippet, voice input will be converted into text and stored in SpeechRecognitionObj. Above code can be embedded into a MDK modal page as below

 

{
  "Caption": "Use Voice or Free Text",
  "ActionBar": {
    "Items": [
      {
        "Position": "left",
        "SystemItem": "Cancel",
        "OnPress": "/MDKDevApp/Actions/ClosePage.action"
      },
      {
        "Position":"right",
        "Caption": "Confirm",
        "OnPress":  "/MDKDevApp/Rules/ImageAnalysis/SubmitTextToEditForm.js"
      }
    ]
  },
  "Controls": [
    {
      "_Type": "Control.Type.SectionedTable",
      "_Name": "SectionedTable1",
      "Sections": [
        {
          "ObjectHeader": {
            "Subhead": "Guided questions: ",
            "Description": "/MDKDevApp/Rules/SpeechAnalysis/GetGuidedQuestions.js"
          },
          "Header": {
            "UseTopPadding": false
          },
          "_Type": "Section.Type.ObjectHeader"
        },
        {
          "_Type": "Section.Type.FormCell",
          "Header": {
            "UseTopPadding": false
          },
          "Footer": {
            "UseBottomPadding": false
          },
          "_Name": "FormCellSection",
          "Controls": [
            {
              "_Type": "Control.Type.FormCell.Note",
              "_Name": "NoteFormCell",
              "IsEditable": true,
              "Enabled": true,
              "MaxNumberOfLines": 0,  
              "PlaceHolder": "Your response to the question for form completion.",      
              "validationProperties": {
                "ValidationMessage": "Validation Message",
                "ValidationMessageColor": "ff0000",
                "SeparatorBackgroundColor": "000000",
                "SeparatorIsHidden": false,
                "ValidationViewBackgroundColor": "fffa00",
                "ValidationViewIsHidden": true,
                "MaxNumberOfLines": 2
              }
            }
          ]
        },
        {
          "_Type": "Section.Type.ButtonTable",
          "Separators": {
            "ControlSeparator": false
          },
          "Header": {
            "UseTopPadding": false
          },
          "Footer": {
            "UseBottomPadding": false
          },
          "Layout": {
            "LayoutType": "Horizontal",
            "HorizontalAlignment": "Center"
          },
          "Buttons": [
            {
              "ButtonType": "Primary",
              "Title": "Record",
              "Image": "sap-icon://microphone",
              "OnPress": "/MDKDevApp/Rules/SpeechAnalysis/StartRecording.js",
              "Visible": "/MDKDevApp/Rules/ImageAnalysis/GetRecordControlVisibility.js",
              "_Name": "Record"
            },
            {
              "ButtonType": "Primary",
              "Semantic": "Negative",
              "Title": "Stop",
              "Image": "sap-icon://stop",
              "OnPress":  "/MDKDevApp/Rules/SpeechAnalysis/StopRecording.js",
              "Visible": "/MDKDevApp/Rules/ImageAnalysis/GetRecordControlVisibility.js",
              "_Name": "Stop"
            }
          ]
        }, {  
          "Header": {
            "UseTopPadding": false
          },
          "_Type": "Section.Type.Image",
          "_Name": "PlaceholderForPadding",
          "Image": "/MDKDevApp/Images/transparent.png",
          "Height": 10
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "_Name": "OperationRecord",
  "_Type": "Page"
}

 

The screenshot of this modal page is as below

You may have a question now: since the converted voice input is just plain text, such as "The water pump was installed in the year 2001, and the water pipe is 10 meters long," it is unstructured data. How should we identify the useful information from the plain text, input it into the form, and submit it to the OData service to create a work order data record? That’s a good question. We will again use the AI Core service to achieve this.

We will call following Javascript function

 

async function convertSpeechIntoStucturedData(context, userInput, schema) {
  const method = "POST";
  const path = "/chat/completions?api-version=2024-02-01";
  const body = {
    messages: [
      {
        role: "system",
        content: `Convert the following speech to structure data as defined in ${schema}. The properties in the schema
            are optional so if the speech does not contain the information on some property, do not include it.`
      },
      {
        role: "user",
        content: userInput
      }
    ],
    max_tokens: 512,
    temperature: 0,
    frequency_penalty: 0,
    presence_penalty: 0,
    functions: [{ name: "format_response", parameters: structuredDataSchema }],
    function_call: { name: "format_response" },
  };
  const headers = {
    "content-type": "application/json",
    "AI-Resource-Group": "default"
  };
  const service = "/MDKDevApp/Services/AzureOpenAI.service";
  console.log("Converting natural language to structured data...")
  return context.executeAction({
    "Name": "/MDKDevApp/Actions/SendRequest.action",
    "Properties": {
      "Target": {
        "Path": path,
        "RequestProperties": {
          "Method": method,
          "Headers": headers,
          "Body": JSON.stringify(body)
        },
        "Service": service
      }
    },
    "ActivityIndicatorText": "Converting natural language to structured data...",
  }).then(response => {
    const resultsObj = JSON.parse(response.data.choices[0].message.function_call.arguments);
    console.log("Structured data results:", resultsObj);
    console.log("");
    return resultsObj;
  });

 

By now, you should be very familiar with this kind of JavaScript function. We have repeatedly used it a few times for meter reading, anomaly detection, maintenance guideline generation, etc.

In the POST request body, the variable ${schema} is the same as the one we saved in the earlier step, the form cells schema. Similarly, as before, we also use an LLM function call format_response to specify the structured output we want from the AI core service. This format_response function has a parameter structuredDataSchema to specify the schema of the structured output we want.

 

const structuredDataSchema = {
  "type": "object",
  "properties": {
    "formCell": {
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Refers to each item in the schema array. The title property must completely match the one defined in schema",
      "items": {
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
          "title": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Title of the property"
          },
          "value": {
            "type": "string",
            "description": "Value of the property"
          }
        },
        "required": ["title", "value"]
      }
    }
  },
  "required": ["formCell"]
};

 

Thus, the AI core service will process the input "The water pump was installed in the year 2001, and the water pipe is 10 meters long," extract the useful information, and adhere to the structuredDataSchema to output the structured data as shown below:

 

Structured data results: {
    "formCell": [
      {
        "title": "yearOfInstallation",
        "value": "2001"
      },
      {
        "title": "length",
        "value": "10 meter"
      }
    ]
  }

 

The output structured data will be populated into the form cells accordingly and submitted to the OData service to create a new work order for equipment operation and maintenance.

To summarize, in this blog, we provide technical details and examples on how to use the SAP AI core service in an MDK app to convert natural language input into structured data with the help of Retrieval Augmented Generation and LLM functions. The structured data is then populated into form cells and submitted to the OData service to create a work order for equipment operation and maintenance.

This marks the temporary end of our series on SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) integration with SAP AI Core service. Thank you for joining me throughout this series. However, this is not the end, but just the beginning of our MDK AI journey. We will continue to update you with new AI-related features for MDK, so stay tuned!

entry.title: Product Updates for SAP Build Code – September 2024 Edition
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

As we wrap up another exciting month, we wanted to take a moment to share some of the latest updates with you. With September coming to a close, we are getting ready for two major events in the SAP Community - Devtoberfest and TechEd. For more information, be sure to read the rest of this release information.

 

Support SAP S/4HANA Cloud Service Integration for Data Model and App Logic

You can use our generative AI assistant Joule in SAP Build Code to help you to write code quickly and easily by providing natural language prompts. As you know, we are constantly listening to your feedback to make our tools even better. One request we got was to enable AI support for external service scenarios. We are excited to tell you that it is now possible to use Joule to generate code for your integrated SAP S/4HANA Cloud services.

You start by creating your full-stack application, importing an external SAP S/4HANA Cloud service, and defining the data model and services. (With Joule, you can easily create your data model and services.)

Data Model and Service creation with the help of Joule

With our new feature, it is now possible to use Joule to enhance the data model you created before: You can add associations between the external entity and local entities, and you can expose the external entity to a local service via a projection.

Using Joule to add an association between the entity Books and the external entity A_BusinessPartner

We've also added support for custom logic generation to read the associated external SAP S/4HANA Cloud entity and to manipulate the exposed external entity.

Using Joule to add a custom logic reading data from the external entity A_BusinessPartner

 

SQL Notebooks for SAP HANA

SAP Build Code offers notebook functionality, similar to the well-known Jupyter notebooks. Since Q3 2024 the SAP HANA tools in SAP Build Code offer a notebook experience for executing SQL statements on an SAP HANA Cloud database.

To get started, create an SAP HANA dev space. Inside the dev space, create a file with the .notebook extension and open it. The file will be opened with the default notebook editor. Next, create a code block. The default code block type should be “SAP HANA Connection Management”. This code block type allows opening, closing, and selecting a database connection. Typing ‘#’ opens the value help with the available commands.

Choose the “List database connections” Command

Choose the “List database connections” command and execute the code block. A list of the available database connections from the SAP HANA Database Explorer view will be shown. You can choose a database connection to use for the notebook by clicking on its name in the list. The selected database will be highlighted with a different background color.

Select Database Connection

Now you can create another code block for executing SQL statements. Be sure to change the code block type to “SAP HANA SQL” by clicking on the “SAP HANA Connection Management” indicator in the bottom right corner and choosing “SAP HANA SQL” from the subsequent dialog.

Execute SQL Queries

Add one or more SQL statements and execute the code block. The results will be shown below the code block.

Add SQL Statements and Execute the Code Block

You can add more code and markdown blocks as needed.

 

Use the Joule functionality to enhance your MDK projects in SAP Build Code

Remember back in June when we announced you could create and manage your mobile development kit (MDK) apps directly from the SAP Build lobby and continue their development using the Storyboard in SAP Build Code? We're thrilled to share another exciting update on that front!

You can now take advantage of the generative AI assistant Joule to enhance your existing MDK projects in SAP Build Code. For further details, I recommend to read this blogpost written by my colleague Jitendra.

 

Devtoberfest and TechEd

Get ready for an exciting journey of learning and collaboration with Devtoberfest and SAP TechEd Virtual!

From September 23rd to October 25th, 2024, join us for Devtoberfest, a celebration of coding and collaboration within the SAP Community. This virtual event is designed to prepare you for the highly anticipated SAP TechEd. My colleague Ravi summarizes all you need to know about SAP Build Code at Devtoberfest. For further information, read the Devtoberfest 2024 Welcome blog post and join the Devtoberfest group in SAP Community to take part in this great event!

And don't forget to mark your calendar for SAP TechEd Virtual on October 8-9, 2024, your gateway to the latest innovations and advancements in SAP technologies! The session catalog is now live, featuring a wide range of topics across various tracks, including the Application Development and Automation and the Data and Analytics tracks, where you'll find dedicated sessions on SAP Build Code. You are looking for a summary where to find SAP Build Code at SAP TechEd? Then I recommend to read this blog post.

Don't miss these opportunities to enhance your technical expertise and connect with the SAP community.

 

Let Us Hear Your Voice!

We prepared a short survey, focused on improving the product to better fit your needs. By participating, you can help us improve SAP Build Code and SAP Business Application Studio. It should only take 5 minutes to complete. Please take the survey now.

 

For further information on new features, please check our roadmaps: roadmap for SAP Build Code, SAPUI5 Road Map. And have a look at What's New in SAP Build Code, the What's New Viewer for SAPUI5 and the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model Release Notes. If you want to stay up-to-date with news, learning resources, and product and strategy updates for SAP Build Code: follow our tag.

We look forward to seeing how you use these new capabilities to create innovative solutions and drive your projects forward. Stay tuned for more updates and happy coding! 😊

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Build – Q3 2024 Edition
entry.content:
Greetings, Builders! We’re pleased to bring you the Q3 Edition of our What’s New in SAP Build series. In this edition, we’re covering product-specific updates for:

  • SAP Build Apps
  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Start, SAP Task Center
  • SAP Build Code

SAP Build Apps

Release management and project lifecycle management: It’s now possible to release and transport projects across different tenants using SAP Cloud Transport Management System. You can transport project versions between subaccounts while isolating development, testing, and productive deployment of SAP Build Apps applications to their own landscapes. To get started, see this blog post.

Performant table list with large dataset from S/4HANA: Managing and displaying large amounts of data in an application can be challenging and cause performance issues. Check out this example of creating a performant list page for an S/4HANA Cloud endpoint with 65k+ records - see the blog post.

Copying pages within one project: This feature enables users to copy and duplicate a page within a project in SAP Build Apps – a simple, yet powerful, add-on to further speed up development.

New Build Service: We recently launched a new and improved experience for deploying application packages. The build service includes managing image assets, iOS usage strings, mobile service enablement, package creation, and configuration management. The updated UI provides a much smoother experience for developers at the deployment phase, ensuring that every requirement is met and that your apps are ready to publish. Try it out today!

  • Also check out Ludo Noens’ series on Consuming SAP with SAP Build Apps – Creating a CRUD Application
  • Learn more about what’s coming up on the SAP Build Apps Roadmap.

SAP Build Process Automation

Script Task Editor: It's now possible to write JavaScript code in the new Script Editor, allowing the execution of complex data type transformations and additional operations on the process context. Previously this was limited to automation - now it includes process inputs and custom variables, facilitating advanced data manipulation and transformation. See more in the blog post.

Scheduled trigger: Now you can schedule process automations based on user-defined time intervals and dates at runtime. This allows you to enable system-centric and time-based process automation use cases, such as working and nonworking days in creating your automation scenarios to avoid resource mismatches.

Additional Outcomes in Forms and UI5 Support for Tasks: This enables citizen developers to configure additional outcome actions in the process to let approvers request rework in case of missing or wrong information in approval forms. You can also start filling out multiple forms efficiently through a guided experience.

Advanced Event Project Creation with Manual Specification Upload: This allows users to upload business event specifications for the creation of an event project in the lobby. The new feature adds support for business events across lines of business (LOBs) and custom events, including third-party events that are supported at runtime using SAP Event Mesh.

Process Visibility enhancements: These include actionable insights, instance tracking for requestors and additional monitoring capabilities via CALM. Now you can turn insights into meaningful outcomes and enable the triggering of escalation flows based on visibility insights, to directly react and resolve critical situations. This enables auditable and governed escalation resolution, instead of having disconnected communication through different channels.

More on SAP Build Process Automation:

  • See the What's New Viewer for more updates, and improvements.
  • See SAP Road Map Explorer for more upcoming features and focus areas.

SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Start, and SAP Task Center

SAP Build Work Zone, both editions

HTML5 Business Solution as a Content Provider: With the latest update in SAP Build Work Zone, you can now expose BTP-based applications as Work Zone content, like roles, spaces, and pages, in the 'Common Data Model' (CDM) format. As a result, you can easily configure and enable access to BTP applications from a Work Zone instance, even across subaccounts. See more in the Help Portal and this blog post.

SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition

Define Sort Order of pages and spaces: Administrators can now configure how spaces and pages are sorted on the menu bar of SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition. This feature is only applicable if you're working in the new experience view mode for spaces and pages. See more in the Help Portal.

Import and Export of Pages and Spaces: Content manager tools will be enhanced to enable administrators to export and import custom (locally created) spaces and pages between landscapes.

SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition

Integrated guided experience across SAP Build Work Zone and Process Automation*: Guided processes are now available as an artifact in SAP Build Process Automation. In addition to the previously available “no template” wizards, it is now possible to create guided, process-based wizards in SAP Build Work Zone.

*Note: This will become GA in October 2024. 

SAP Start

Directly navigate to product home pages of integrated cloud solutions: The SAP Start site offers a new product section to easily see and navigate to the product home pages of the integrated solutions. This enables transparency of available cloud solutions for the current user, and quick and easy navigation to launch the product home page of the desired solution.

SAP Task Center

ITCE partner page: SAP Task Center now allows connections with third-party task providers.
